user_story: As a user, I want to be able to check transaction history and keep a record of it, so that I can go back when needed.
llm_output:
### (1) Gherkin Scenarios in .feature Format

```gherkin
Feature: Transaction History

  Scenario: User views transaction history
    Given the user is logged into their account
    When the user navigates to the transaction history page
    Then the user should see a list of past transactions

  Scenario: User downloads transaction history
    Given the user is logged into their account
    When the user navigates to the transaction history page
    And the user clicks on the "Download" button
    Then the user should receive a file containing the transaction history
```

### (2) Step Definitions in Python

```python
from behave import given, when, then

@given('the user is logged into their account')
def step_impl(context):
    context.browser.get('https://example.com/login')
    context.browser.find_element_by_id('username').send_keys('user')
    context.browser.find_element_by_id('password').send_keys('password')
    context.browser.find_element_by_id('login-button').click()

@when('the user navigates to the transaction history page')
def step_impl(context):
    context.browser.get('https://example.com/transaction-history')

@then('the user should see a list of past transactions')
def step_impl(context):
    transactions = context.browser.find_elements_by_class_name('transaction')
    assert len(transactions) > 0

@when('the user clicks on the "Download" button')
def step_impl(context):
    context.browser.find_element_by_id('download-button').click()

@then('the user should receive a file containing the transaction history')
def step_impl(context):
    # Assuming the file is downloaded to a predefined directory
    import os
    import time

    download_dir = '/path/to/downloads'
    time.sleep(5)  # Wait for the download to complete
    files = os.listdir(download_dir)
    assert any(file.endswith('.csv') for file in files)
```

### (3) Validation Checklist

- [ ] Verify that the user can log into their account.
- [ ] Verify that the user can navigate to the transaction history page.
- [ ] Verify that the transaction history page displays a list of past transactions.
- [ ] Verify that the user can click on the "Download" button.
- [ ] Verify that a file containing the transaction history is downloaded.
- [ ] Verify that the downloaded file is in the correct format (e.g., .csv).
- [ ] Verify that the downloaded file contains the correct transaction data.

user_story: As a Researcher, I want an app that generates an OpenRefine reconciliation API endpoint from a Tabular Data Package, so that I can use it to clean messy data.
As a developer, I want an DSpace integration, so that I can integrate data-packaged data with pipelines that use Dspace.

As a user, I want to be able to browse through the list of facolities and see which ones are environment-friendly, so that I can know for sure my waste is not going to leave a negative ecological footprint.
llm_output:
### (1) Gherkin Scenarios in .feature format

```gherkin
Feature: Browse Environment-Friendly Facilities

  Scenario: User views the list of facilities
    Given the user is on the facilities page
    When the user browses through the list of facilities
    Then the user should see a list of facilities

  Scenario: User sees environment-friendly facilities
    Given the user is on the facilities page
    When the user browses through the list of facilities
    Then the user should see which facilities are environment-friendly
```

### (2) Step Definitions in Python

```python
from behave import given, when, then

@given('the user is on the facilities page')
def step_given_user_on_facilities_page(context):
    context.browser.get('http://example.com/facilities')

@when('the user browses through the list of facilities')
def step_when_user_browses_facilities(context):
    context.facilities_list = context.browser.find_elements_by_class_name('facility')

@then('the user should see a list of facilities')
def step_then_user_sees_list_of_facilities(context):
    assert len(context.facilities_list) > 0

@then('the user should see which facilities are environment-friendly')
def step_then_user_sees_environment_friendly_facilities(context):
    environment_friendly_facilities = [facility for facility in context.facilities_list if 'environment-friendly' in facility.get_attribute('class')]
    assert len(environment_friendly_facilities) > 0
```

### (3) Validation Checklist

- [ ] Verify that the user can navigate to the facilities page.
- [ ] Verify that the facilities page displays a list of facilities.
- [ ] Verify that each facility in the list is displayed correctly.
- [ ] Verify that environment-friendly facilities are clearly marked.
- [ ] Verify that the marking for environment-friendly facilities is consistent and visible.
- [ ] Verify that the list of facilities updates correctly when new facilities are added.
- [ ] Verify that the user can distinguish between environment-friendly and non-environment-friendly facilities.

user_story: As a depositor, I want to link datasets with the project DMP, so that compliance with DMP can be demonstrated and whole project workflow is linked together.
As an Applicant, I want to Complete Building Development Project, so that I can receive any posted bond and/or obtain occupancy.
llm_output:
### (1) Gherkin Scenarios in .feature format

```gherkin
Feature: Complete Building Development Project

  Scenario: Applicant completes the building development project
    Given the applicant has a building development project
    When the applicant marks the project as complete
    Then the system should validate the project completion
    And the system should release any posted bond
    And the system should update the occupancy status
```

### (2) Step Definitions in Python

```python
from behave import given, when, then

@given('the applicant has a building development project')
def step_impl(context):
    context.project = get_applicant_project()
    assert context.project is not None

@when('the applicant marks the project as complete')
def step_impl(context):
    context.project.mark_as_complete()

@then('the system should validate the project completion')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.project.is_validated()

@then('the system should release any posted bond')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.project.bond_released()

@then('the system should update the occupancy status')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.project.occupancy_status_updated()
```

### (3) Validation Checklist

- [ ] Ensure the applicant has a building development project.
- [ ] Verify the project can be marked as complete by the applicant.
- [ ] Validate the project completion status.
- [ ] Confirm the system releases any posted bond upon project completion.
- [ ] Check that the system updates the occupancy status after project completion.

user_story: As a anonymoususer, I want to have the training options clearly marked for the following information: day and time of training, title, short summary, trainers, level, number of registrations, remaining registrations, location of training, link to full details of the training, register now link, so that I can shop for training all with one page AND find their class on on one page.
As an Agency user, I want to accurately see who created a submission, so that I'm not confused about who last updated a submission.
llm_output:
### (1) Gherkin Scenarios in .feature format

```gherkin
Feature: View submission creator
  As an Agency user,
  I want to accurately see who created a submission,
  So that I'm not confused about who last updated a submission.

  Scenario: View creator of a newly created submission
    Given I am logged in as an Agency user
    When I create a new submission
    Then I should see my name as the creator of the submission

  Scenario: View creator of an existing submission
    Given I am logged in as an Agency user
    And there is an existing submission created by another user
    When I view the submission
    Then I should see the name of the user who created the submission

  Scenario: View creator after submission update
    Given I am logged in as an Agency user
    And there is an existing submission created by another user
    When I update the submission
    Then I should still see the name of the user who created the submission
```

### (2) Step Definitions in Python

```python
from behave import given, when, then

@given('I am logged in as an Agency user')
def step_impl(context):
    context.user = login_as_agency_user()

@when('I create a new submission')
def step_impl(context):
    context.submission = create_new_submission(context.user)

@then('I should see my name as the creator of the submission')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.submission.creator == context.user.name

@given('there is an existing submission created by another user')
def step_impl(context):
    context.other_user = create_user('other_user')
    context.submission = create_new_submission(context.other_user)

@when('I view the submission')
def step_impl(context):
    context.viewed_submission = view_submission(context.submission.id)

@then('I should see the name of the user who created the submission')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.viewed_submission.creator == context.other_user.name

@when('I update the submission')
def step_impl(context):
    context.updated_submission = update_submission(context.submission.id, context.user)

@then('I should still see the name of the user who created the submission')
def step_impl(context):
    assert context.updated_submission.creator == context.other_user.name
```

### (3) Validation Checklist

- [ ] Verify that the user is logged in as an Agency user.
- [ ] Verify that a new submission shows the logged-in user as the creator.
- [ ] Verify that an existing submission shows the correct creator's name.
- [ ] Verify that updating a submission does not change the creator's name.
- [ ] Verify that the creator's name is displayed correctly in the submission view.
- [ ] Verify that the system handles multiple users creating and updating submissions without confusion.
- [ ] Verify that the creator's name is stored and retrieved accurately from the database.

user_story: As a trainer, I want to copy one of my courses or events, so that I can create a new one.
llm_output:
### (1) Gherkin Scenarios in .feature format

```gherkin
Feature: Copy Course or Event
  As a trainer,
  I want to copy one of my courses or events,
  So that I can create a new one.

  Scenario: Copy a course
    Given I am logged in as a trainer
    And I have a course named "Course A"
    When I choose to copy "Course A"
    Then a new course named "Copy of Course A" should be created
    And the new course should have the same content as "Course A"

  Scenario: Copy an event
    Given I am logged in as a trainer
    And I have an event named "Event A"
    When I choose to copy "Event A"
    Then a new event named "Copy of Event A" should be created
    And the new event should have the same content as "Event A"
```

### (2) Step Definitions in Python

```python
from behave import given, when, then

@given('I am logged in as a trainer')
def step_impl(context):
    context.trainer = Trainer()
    context.trainer.login()

@given('I have a course named "{course_name}"')
def step_impl(context, course_name):
    context.course = Course(name=course_name)
    context.trainer.add_course(context.course)

@given('I have an event named "{event_name}"')
def step_impl(context, event_name):
    context.event = Event(name=event_name)
    context.trainer.add_event(context.event)

@when('I choose to copy "{name}"')
def step_impl(context, name):
    if hasattr(context, 'course') and context.course.name == name:
        context.new_course = context.trainer.copy_course(context.course)
    elif hasattr(context, 'event') and context.event.name == name:
        context.new_event = context.trainer.copy_event(context.event)

@then('a new course named "Copy of {course_name}" should be created')
def step_impl(context, course_name):
    assert context.new_course.name == f"Copy of {course_name}"

@then('a new event named "Copy of {event_name}" should be created')
def step_impl(context, event_name):
    assert context.new_event.name == f"Copy of {event_name}"

@then('the new course should have the same content as "{course_name}"')
def step_impl(context, course_name):
    assert context.new_course.content == context.course.content

@then('the new event should have the same content as "{event_name}"')
def step_impl(context, event_name):
    assert context.new_event.content == context.event.content
```

### (3) Validation Checklist

- [ ] Verify that the trainer can log in successfully.
- [ ] Verify that the trainer has a course named "Course A".
- [ ] Verify that the trainer has an event named "Event A".
- [ ] Verify that the trainer can choose to copy a course.
- [ ] Verify that the trainer can choose to copy an event.
- [ ] Verify that a new course named "Copy of Course A" is created.
- [ ] Verify that a new event named "Copy of Event A" is created.
- [ ] Verify that the new course has the same content as the original course.
- [ ] Verify that the new event has the same content as the original event.
